news.title: China's Alibaba targets $100B in AI and cloud revenue over 5 years
news.summary: China’s technology giant Alibaba Group pledged on Thursday a goal of surpassing $100 billion in revenue from its artificial intelligence and cloud businesses over the next five years, which it said would be powered by the AI demand boom.  For the October-December quarter, the company, which shifted its focus to cloud and AI technologies in recent years, reported an overall revenue increase of 2% year-on-year to 284.8 billion yuan ($41.4 billion), lower than analysts’ estimates.  CEO Eddie Wu said during an earnings call on Thursday that Alibaba stands to benefit from the “exponential growth in AI demand.”

news.title: Alibaba stock falls despite AI outlook, Accenture lowers guidance
news.summary: Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre and Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman take a closer look at some of Thursday morning's trending tickers and stories. Alibaba (BABA) stock is plunging after the company reported revenue that fell short of expectations, though analysts are still bullish on Alibaba's artificial intelligence (AI) outlook. Accenture (ACN) lowered its full-year guidance. Elliott Investment Management is building a stake in Invisalign-maker Align Technology (ALGN).
